articles.title: Oil Holds Gain as Iran Seeks Hormuz Control Before Talks With US
articles.summary: Oil held an advance before expected US-Iran talks in Doha, as conflicting signals from Washington and Tehran cast doubt over the next phase of negotiations to end the four-month war.

articles.title: Morgan Stanley Cuts Oil Forecasts on Fast Return of Hormuz Flows
articles.summary: Morgan Stanley cuts its oil price forecasts as flows through the Strait of Hormuz return faster than expected, while strong US supply and weak Chinese demand increase the risk of a surplus.

articles.title: China Loses Out on AI Boom as Stocks Trail by Most Since ‘01
articles.summary: After a banner year for Chinese stocks on the back of AI advances, 2026 is not going well.

articles.title: Asian Stocks Climb on Tech, Yen Hits 40-Year Low: Markets Wrap
articles.summary: Asian equities rose, leaving the regional benchmark on track for its best quarterly gain in 17 years as technology stocks rallied. The yen weakened to a four-decade low against the dollar.

articles.title: Gold Holds Drop as US-Iran Tensions Weigh on Inflation Outlook
articles.summary: Gold held a decline as traders weighed conflicting signals from the US and Iran ahead of fresh talks to end the war that’s fueled global inflationary pressures.

articles.title: Yen Hits Four-Decade Low in Historic Slide That’s Rattled Japan
articles.summary: The yen slid to its weakest level against the dollar since 1986, a milestone that will generate unease in Japan and put traders on high alert for authorities wading into the market.

articles.title: Taiwan Dollar Set to Face Pressure From Record Dividend Payouts
articles.summary: The Taiwan dollar, which is on track for its first quarterly gain in a year, may face renewed pressure as the US currency strengthens and local companies boost dividend payouts to a record.

articles.title: Raizen Burns More Cash, Sets Steeper Charge Amid Restructure

articles.title: Egypt Edges Closer to $1.6 Billion IMF Payout With Initial Deal
articles.summary: The International Monetary Fund and Egypt reached a preliminary deal on the country’s latest program review, putting Cairo on track to unlock more than $1.6 billion in financing.
